name: Chambers v. Mississippi
question: Is there a violation of the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment when the defendant cannot cross-examine a witness or admit the testimony of another person’s confession of the crime to a third party?
description: A case in which the Court ruled that it is a violation of the Due Process Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment when the defendant cannot cross-examine a witness or admit the testimony of another person’s confession of the crime to a third party.
